_schema.description: Search results data for the e-shop prototype. Add a new key under `queries` for each test query you want to render with real data. Anything not listed falls back to `default` which renders placeholder (strike-through) content for usability testing.

shape.totalResults: number — shown next to 'Search results for ...'
shape.goods: array of { name, price, vatNote, stock: 'ok'|'low'|'none', badge, image }
shape.articles: array of { title, description, badges, action }

highlighted.title: Hotend PTFE tube (MK2.5S, MK3S, MMU2S)
highlighted.description: Replacement PTFE tube for the hotend on MK2.5S/MK3S/MMU2S. Polytetrafluoroethylene with low friction coefficient — guides filament cleanly from the heatbreak to the nozzle.

goods.name: Hotend PTFE tube (MK2/S, MK2.5, MK3, MMU2)

articles.title: PTFE tubes in Original Prusa i3 printers
articles.description: Why PTFE — low friction guides filament cleanly. Different tubes used in single- vs. multi-material printers, when to replace, where to source spare tubes (Festo or e-shop).

articles.title: How to trim PTFE tube - Original Prusa printers
articles.description: Step-by-step guide to cut your own PTFE tube to the right length. Covers tools, square-cut technique, and how to chamfer the ends to avoid filament hang-ups.

blog.title: Prusa Printer Enclosure V2 - with MMU2S support
blog.description: Updated Prusa enclosure accommodating MK3S + MMU2S unit with PTFE tube routing for clean multi-material printing. Stable temperatures, better adhesion for ABS / ASA.

usefulLinks.title: How to replace a hotend PTFE tube

articles.title: How to install the Nextruder V6 Nozzle Adapter (MK4/MK3.9)
articles.description: Step-by-step guide to install the V6 adapter on MK4/MK3.9. Covers hotend protection, removal, and final torque settings. Lets you swap to any V6-compatible nozzle.

articles.title: How to install the Nextruder V6 Nozzle Adapter (MK4S/MK3.9S)
articles.description: MK4S/MK3.9S-specific guide for the V6 adapter. Updated procedure for the new heaterblock and high-flow hotend variants.

blog.title: Original Prusa MK4 is here: Always perfect first layer, high-speed printing with Input shaper, 32-bit platform, Nextruder, MMU3. SHIPPING NOW!
blog.description: The launch post for the MK4 — introduces the Nextruder, load-cell first-layer calibration, 32-bit xBuddy board, Input Shaper, and Pressure Advance. The original Nextruder reveal.

Nextruder Hotend brass High Flow - 0.60 mm

articles.title: Hotend — extruder componentry
articles.description: What the hotend is, the parts that make it up (heatbreak / heaterblock / heatsink / heater cartridge / thermistor / nozzle), and how Nextruder differs from E3D V6.

articles.title: Hotend fan
articles.description: Role of the hotend fan: keeps the heatsink cool to prevent heat creep. Difference between hotend fan and print (part-cooling) fan, when to replace it, and how.

blog.title: Everything about nozzles with a different diameter
blog.description: Companion piece for nozzle and hotend swaps. Smaller diameters (0.25mm) for fine detail, larger (0.6/0.8mm) for speed and strength. Includes recommended PrusaSlicer settings.

highlighted.title: Prusa Nozzle brass CHT - 0.4 mm
highlighted.description: All-metal high-precision high-flow nozzle with CHT (Core Heating Technology) for the Nextruder. Combines nozzle and heatbreak in one piece, dramatically improves flow and print speed.

goods.name: E3D Prusa Nozzle: High Flow ObXidian™ - 0.4 mm

articles.title: Nozzle — filament extrusion component
articles.description: What a nozzle does, how brass/hardened/CHT differ, and when to swap diameter. Glossary entry covering all Prusa printer families.

articles.title: Changing/replacing the nozzle (MINI)
articles.description: Step-by-step replacement for the Original Prusa MINI. Requires 16mm spanner + 7mm socket. Includes calibration steps after the swap.

blog.title: Nozzle diameter — changing and benefits
blog.description: Everything about nozzles with a different diameter: 0.25mm for detail, 0.6mm for speed, 0.8mm for strength. Side-by-side comparisons, sample prints, recommended use cases.

highlighted.title: Prusament PLA Pristine White 900g Refill (NFC Compatible)
highlighted.description: Eco-friendly refill spool for Prusament PLA — keeps your existing spool body, swaps the filament in seconds. ±0.02mm precision, fully rewritable NFC tag.

goods.name: Prusament PLA Pristine White 900g Refill (NFC Compatible)

articles.title: PLA filament — properties and print settings
articles.description: PLA is the easiest filament to print: low warping, no fumes, reliable first layer. Recommended nozzle 185–235 °C and bed 50–60 °C. Best for prototypes and decorative parts.

articles.title: Prusament PLA — Material Guide
articles.description: Full material guide for Prusament PLA: temperatures, retraction, cooling, supports, post-processing. Includes recommended PrusaSlicer profiles for all printer families.

blog.title: Introducing Prusament PLA Recycled!
blog.description: 100% recycled Prusament PLA — made from production waste, unique color per batch, 2kg spool. Slightly looser tolerance (±0.05mm) but solid printing performance.

highlighted.description: Multi-material upgrade for CORE One — print up to 5 colors or materials in one job. Open-frame variant, easy installation, and full PrusaSlicer integration.

articles.title: MMU3 Compatibility
articles.description: Complete compatibility matrix: which Prusa printers support MMU3, which firmware versions are required, and how to migrate from MMU2S setups.

blog.title: Make your prints shine with colors! The MMU3 for the Prusa CORE One is Here!
blog.description: Multi-material printing comes to CORE One. Two MMU3 variants (Lite and Enclosed), seamless filament switching, and ready-to-print color profiles. Order now.

blog.description: Why filament moisture matters, how to choose a drybox, and DIY alternatives. We cover hygrometer placement, desiccant types, and storage best practices for nylon, PETG and TPU.

blog.title: Prusa CORE One INDX — Unmatched Toolchanger With 8 Nozzles — Orders Now Open!
blog.description: Today we're opening orders for the INDX conversion kit. Eight independent toolheads, automatic tool docking, and seamless PrusaSlicer integration. Here's everything you need to know.

blog.description: A deep-dive into what makes INDX different from MMU3 and other multi-material systems. Build quality, speed comparisons, and material profiles.

highlighted.description: The next evolution of our loved CoreXY printer — ready for INDX. Auto material switching, faster prints, quieter operation.

articles.description: Improved Input Shaper auto-tuning, chamber temperature control, and new pause-mid-print behavior for filament changes.

articles.title: ABS, ASA and Nylon on CORE One — Chamber Temperature Guide
articles.description: CORE One heats chamber passively to 55 °C. Settings for engineering-grade materials and how to tune per filament brand.

articles.title: Calibration and maintenance schedule for CORE One
articles.description: Recommended monthly and quarterly checks: belt tension, idler condition, bearing lubrication, and Z-axis re-leveling.

blog.title: Exploring CORE One+: New Features, Upgrades, INDX
blog.description: A deep-dive into what makes the CORE One+ different from CORE One. AMS-style material switching, chamber control, and the upcoming INDX dock.

blog.title: Behind the design of CORE One — interview with the engineering team
